content.text: The cryptocurrency market includes a variety of assets with different price points. Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) is currently valued at $80,488.74, reflecting its close relationship with Bitcoin. Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) is priced at $2,018.31, maintaining its role as a tokenized version of Ethereum. Solana (SOL) is trading at $126.42, continuing to be a key player in the blockchain ecosystem. Meanwhile, ai16z, a lesser-known asset, is priced at $0.1925.
A fix has been implemented to resolve a missing moment rollup external issue in the-org build.
Core types have been added to enable the generation of index.d.ts in the /dist directory, addressing errors related to missing types in imports.

A user is experiencing issues with pnpm install and build while switching from eliza-starter to the main eliza repository. The error indicates a failure to switch pnpm to version 9.15.7, with missing CLI files causing build failures in multiple packages. Despite forcing pnpm to switch versions, the issue persists.

categories.title: Issues with Twitter Plugin and Telegram Plugin Fix

content.text: A user reported an issue with the '@elizaos-plugins/client-twitter' plugin, which fails to process Twitter actions despite all plugins working correctly in previous launches. The issue persists regardless of whether 'ragKnowledge' is enabled or disabled. Logs indicate warnings and errors related to deprecated modules and invalid embedding inputs. Additionally, an error message states 'Error processing tweet undefined.'

A fix was introduced for the '@elizaos-plugins/client-telegram' plugin by adding a 'serviceType' property. This change allows multi-platform support for Ruby, enabling cross-platform testing. The fix consists of a single line of code defining 'static serviceType = TELEGRAM_SERVICE_NAME'.
A fix was implemented to address a missing moment rollup external dependency in the ElizaOS build system.
Core types were added to enable the generation of `index.d.ts` in the `/dist` directory, resolving missing type errors in imports.
A user reported being unable to complete `pnpm install` and build due to an issue with switching to `pnpm v9.15.7`. The error persisted despite manually forcing the version switch.

A tutorial on building a Social AI Agent in 15 minutes was found to be outdated. The embedded video and textual instructions no longer align with the current implementation. Additionally, inconsistencies in the documentation regarding Twitter as a client or plugin were noted.
A user encountered an 'Error processing tweet undefined' issue when using the Twitter plugin. The error persisted despite ensuring the fork was up to date and checking various configurations.
A fix was introduced for NEAR AI image generation, ensuring the correct API invocation and preventing fallback to OpenAI.
A minor fix was made to the Telegram plugin, adding `serviceType` to support multi-platform functionality for Ruby.
Six contributors merged two pull requests, focusing on various improvements.
